page: Amazon Web Services m8id.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated virtual CPUs (16 physical cores with hyper-threading) operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Local storage is provided via a 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and network connectivity is rated at a baseline of 15 Gbps. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8id.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance across multiple domains. Its single-core capabilities are highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2208.0 and a bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 14.54 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. Multi-core performance is also robust, with a Passmark CPU score of 51493.13 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 18435.0. Memory subsystem performance is notable, achieving a read-only large block bandwidth of 179.94 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the hardware-accelerated Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) contribute to strong LLM inference speeds, reaching 374.61 tokens/sec for text generation on small models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8id.8xlarge offers high resource density by bundling fast DDR5 memory with a high-capacity local NVMe SSD. This integration reduces the need for external block storage attachments for high-throughput operations, though the local storage is ephemeral. The absence of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, but its strong CPU-based matrix instructions offset this for smaller inference tasks. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for database operations, Redis caching layers, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring fast local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 128 GB of DDR5 memory. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 15 Gbps baseline network. The instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2208.0 and a bzip3 decompression speed of 14.54 GB/sec. It also exhibits strong multi-core capabilities with a Passmark CPU score of 51493.13 and a Redis SET performance of 58.94 million operations per second. While the lack of a dedicated GPU restricts heavy parallel training, its Intel AMX support enables efficient CPU-based LLM inference. The m8id.8xlarge is well-suited for high-throughput databases, web serving, software compilation, and caching workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 1900 GB NVMe SSD local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.8xlarge features 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 1900 GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-performance workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory, fast local NVMe storage, and top-tier single-core Intel Xeon performance.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs operating at 3.9 GHz with 480 MB L3 cache.
128 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory and 15 Gbps baseline networking.
1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage for high-speed read/write operations.
Top-tier single-core performance with a Geekbench score of 2208.0.
Intel AMX support accelerating CPU-based LLM inference and matrix operations.
Optimized for databases, Redis caching, and high-throughput web serving.
